mission.title: opentelemetry-operator: Operator enters CrashLoopBackOff  after multiple errors
mission.description: Operator enters CrashLoopBackOff  after multiple errors. This issue affects 8+ users.

steps.title: Identify opentelemetry-operator troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your opentelemetry-operator deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator -n opentelemetry-operator --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review opentelemetry-operator configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opentelemetry-operator configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator
kubectl get configmap -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentelemetry-operator
```
We're running the latest version of the operator — `v0.113.0` via `v0.74.2` of the operator Helm chart — but have a number of issues that are causing the operator to crashloop.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Operator enters CrashLoopBackOff  after multiple errors
steps.description:
`ghcr.io/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator/opentelemetry-operator:main:145216a3debc585a182e16915dd128364737a3e758f14de0826080394afe795d` has the fix. But we ran into another issue that operator wasn't able to list  Deployment: `no deployments found with the specified label`.

Fix for that is to
```yaml
manager:
  collectorImage:
    repository: "otel/opentelemetry-collector-contrib"

  serviceMonitor:
    enabled: true

  prometheusRule:
    enabled: true
    defaultRules:
      enabled: true

admissionWebhooks:
  certManager:
    issuerRef:
      group: cert-manager.io
      kind: ClusterIssuer
      name: internal-legacy-issuer
```

steps.title: Confirm Operator enters CrashLoopBackOff  after multiple… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator -n opentelemetry-operator --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n opentelemetry-operator --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
`ghcr.io/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator/opentelemetry-operator:main:145216a3debc585a182e16915dd128364737a3e758f14de0826080394afe795d` has the fix. But we ran into another issue that operator wasn't able to list  Deployment: `no deployments found with the specified label`.

Fix for that is to make sure below labels exist on operator Deployment, as it's fixed
codeSnippets:
manager:
  collectorImage:
    repository: "otel/opentelemetry-collector-contrib"

  serviceMonitor:
    enabled: true

  prometheusRule:
    enabled: true
    defaultRules:
      enabled: true

admissionWebhooks:
  certManager:
    issuerRef:
      group: cert-manager.io
      kind: ClusterIssuer
      name: internal-legacy-issuer

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opentelemetry-operator installed or the issue environment reproducible.
